mnemonics: CUSS & Describe: Use Center, Unusual features, Spread, and Shape to describe any distribution.
keyPoints: The shape of a distribution (e.g., normal, skewed, bimodal) reveals patterns and potential underlying processes in the data.
Measures of center (mean, median) and spread (variance, standard deviation, IQR) quantify the location and variability of the data.
Visual tools like histograms, box plots, and Q-Q plots are essential for exploring and understanding data distributions before formal modeling.
commonMistakes: Assuming data is normally distributed without checking, leading to incorrect model choices and invalid inferences.
Using the mean instead of the median to describe the center of a skewed distribution, which is sensitive to outliers and misleading.
Ignoring the scale of the data when comparing the spread (variance) between different groups or datasets.
